top_stories.title: BSP to finally enforce tighter capital buffer rules on banks
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) will now require banks to build extra capital even during periods of normal credit expansion, a move aimed at strengthening lenders’ buffers and ensuring continued lending during economic downturns. Under Circular No. 1235 dated May 20, the central bank est...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This belongs in the top group because it points to a live shift in banking-system behavior, not just a company headline.

top_stories.title: Banks to set aside capital to support lending during stress
top_stories.summary: PDate: 5/22/2026 Content: ​The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has approved a reform that enables banks to maintain releasable capital, known as the Positive Neutral Countercyclical Capital Buffer (PN-CCyB). Unlike minimum capital requirements, which banks must maintain at all times, the PN-CCyB can be built up du...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This matters because policy pressure can change how banks lend, preserve liquidity, price risk, or compete for deposits.

top_stories.title: PH financial system resources as of March topped P37T
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Philippine financial system’s total resources returned to the P37-trillion level in the first quarter, as banks continued to fuel growth through lending and higher deposits despite market volatility and economic uncertainty stemming from the conflict in the Middle East. secondary_signals.title: Banks told to beef up funds for lending
secondary_signals.summary: Banks are now required to set aside capital that can be tapped to support lending during periods of stress – a reform approved by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) amid heightened global risks and uncertainties arising from the US-Iran conflict.
